title: biscutella longifolia subsp. hungarica
intro: Biscutella longifolia subsp. hungarica, also known as Hungarian biscuit-root or Hungarian cress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, primarily used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Biscutella longifolia subsp. hungarica include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, it can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional folk remedies to soothe respiratory issues.
